subtitle: A 14-kilometer tidal mudflat on the Gulf of Khambhat where roughly a third of the world's end-of-life ships are run aground and dismantled by hand — the largest ship-breaking yard on Earth.
spike_statement: The world's largest ship graveyard: about a third of Earth's retired cargo ships are driven aground on a 13-meter tide here and torn apart by hand.

levers.photo_tone: rust-streaked steel hulls grounded on gray tidal mud under hazy Gujarat heat, cutting torches and cranes against the half-dismantled ships, the human scale of the workers tiny against the bows
